item.text: I’ll stay strictly inside the workspace and begin with `solver_index.md`. I’ll use only the documented harness/perception surface, keep clone probes bounded and symbolic, then implement the level through reusable legs.

item.text: The initial harness confirms level 1 is untouched. I’m now reducing the frame to components and single-action deltas so we can identify the avatar and action semantics without dumping the raw grid.

The frame resolves into a movable black-and-blue figure, a stationary purple-and-blue figure, and a shrinking top timer. Actions 1–4 translate the black figure; action 5 rotates it; action 6 is inert at the start. The stationary figure has a different orientation, so I’m using distance plus orientation/alignment as dense progress and checking reward only at candidate contacts.

item.text: A bounded clone search confirmed the inferred mechanic with a 14-action reward path: close the vertical and horizontal gap, then rotate three quarter-turns so the two figures’ blue contact points meet. I’m encoding translation and rotation as generic legs, with the level player only composing those legs.
